text.title: Seated Mode, Hearing Accessibility, Cognitive Load, and Comfort Settings
text.summary: Seated mode implementation for wheelchair users, 3D caption design for deaf users, cognitive load reduction, and comfort/safety settings for motion sensitivity.

section.title: Seated Mode

rule.statement: Default to seated mode and teleportation-only locomotion; promote standing and smooth locomotion as opt-in settings, not the other way around.
rule.rationale: Wheelchair users, users with limited mobility, and vestibular-sensitive users are excluded by default room-scale and smooth-locomotion experiences. Opt-in seated mode signals they are an afterthought; opt-in standing mode does not.

example.code:
Seated mode adjustments: - Lower UI elements to eye level when seated (~1.2m from floor) - Closer interaction distances (arm's length from seated position) - Reduce vertical range: height -30 to +20 degrees from eye level - All controls within horizontal reach zone (+-90 degrees from center)
Locomotion defaults: - Teleportation-only (point and click to move) - No room-scale movement required - No standing up, overhead reaches, or floor interactions - Virtual joystick and voice commands as alternatives

list.item: Never require room-scale movement, standing, reaching above 1.8m or below 0.5m, or 360-degree rotation.
Snap-turn default: 30 degrees per step; adjustable from 15 to 90 degrees.
Seated-mode preset values are platform-specific — do not transfer Vision Pro values directly to Quest 3 or HoloLens.

section.reference: Python script auditing scene manifest for seated compatibility and other XAUR requirements

rule.statement: 3D captions must always face the user, include speaker identification, note sound direction (e.g., "[footsteps, behind-left]"), maintain a constant ~1.5m distance, and have a clear background.
rule.rationale: Static caption placement in XR fails as users turn their heads. Caption text that reads "footsteps" without direction information provides no spatial context to deaf users.

list.item: Captions at constant ~1.5m distance from user, always facing camera (billboard render mode).
Include: speech content, speaker identification, significant non-speech sounds, directional cues.
Adjustable caption size and position in user settings.
Haptic feedback for important sounds: vibration intensity matches volume, patterns distinguish sound types.
Visual flash indicators for alerts and alarms (non-flicker: max 3 flashes per second).
Leverage platform captions first: visionOS 2+ Live Captions, Meta Horizon OS Live Captions.

antipattern.description: Adding focus highlight animations for accessibility without checking for flicker/strobe risk.
antipattern.reason: More than 3 flashes per second can trigger photosensitive seizures. All animated focus highlights must be checked against the 3-flash threshold.

list.item: Simplified UI mode: hide non-essential elements on demand.
Consistent button placement and gesture vocabulary across scenes.
Tutorial reminders and on-screen hints available on demand.
No time pressure in any core interaction path.

rule.statement: Add vignette during all locomotion and expose a user-adjustable intensity slider; never remove motion sickness mitigations to "improve immersion".
rule.rationale: Vestibular disorders affect ~35% of adults over 40. Smooth locomotion without vignette is a leading cause of simulator sickness that ends XR sessions. Always opt-out, never opt-in for comfort mitigations.

list.item: Motion sickness: teleportation default; vignette on movement; fixed reference frame available; adjustable movement speed.
Visual comfort: brightness and contrast adjustment; reduced particle effects option; field-of-view adjustment; disable motion blur.
Break reminders: suggest break every 20-30 minutes; health and safety warnings on launch; fatigue warnings after sustained use.
